page_content:
EDDI - Education Dialogues

EDDI300 Intergroup Dialogue Facilitator Development & Practicum (3 Credits) Facilitation is an artform and a science. This course is designed to introduce students to foundational dialogue facilitation skills and social justice frameworks needed to effectively facilitate group interactions across and within social differences. Students will apply the skills learned by facilitating a multi-session dialogue course with either the Common Ground or the Words of Engagement Intergroup Dialogue Program (WeIDP). Cross-listed with: CPSP300, HONR300. Restriction: Permission of the Counseling, Higher Education, and Special Education department. Credit Only Granted for: WEID300, CPSP300, HONR300, or EDDI300. Formerly: WEID300. Additional Information: Students must be given permission to enroll and be admitted to the intergroup dialogue facilitator program.

EDCP298 Special Problems in Counseling and Personnel Services (1-3 Credits) Individual instruction in special problems related to counseling, student leadership, and college student development. Restriction: Permission of EDUC-Counseling, Higher Education and Special Education department.

EDCP312 Multi-Ethnic Peer Counseling (3 Credits) Knowledge, skills, and attitude to function as peer helpers of Multi- Ethnic students. Restriction: Sophomore standing or higher. Formerly: EDCP310A.
